title: corylus pumila
intro: Corylus pumila, also known as the dwarf or shrubby hazel, is a small, deciduous shrub native to northern Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Corylus pumila include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to make teas, salves, and other topical applications.
